turns.user_message: I just spent twenty minutes staring at a blank page trying to write down a few simple thoughts, deleting every sentence before I could even finish it. It feels ridiculous to get so hung up on something so basic, but my brain just refuses to cooperate. I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch, yet the frustration lingers long after I finally close the notebook. I wish I could just let things flow naturally instead of picking apart every single word until it sounds completely wrong.

This same cycle has been playing out in my head for months now, turning even the smallest tasks into exhausting mental marathons. I constantly catch myself second-guessing my own instincts, convinced that whatever I produce will inevitably fall short of what anyone else expects. The initial hesitation has slowly morphed into a heavy routine where I brace myself for disappointment before I even begin. It is exhausting to carry this quiet weight around every single day, watching my confidence quietly shrink while I pretend everything is fine.

I am starting to believe that this constant self-editing is just proof that I fundamentally lack the ability to get anything right. Every blank page now feels like a glaring mirror reflecting all my shortcomings back at me, and I hate how paralyzed I become. I try to push through the noise, but the inner voice just grows louder until I am completely convinced that my best efforts are never actually good enough. It is suffocating to feel so thoroughly inadequate over something so simple, trapped in a loop where I can never quite measure up to my own standards.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This has been going on for a while.
